mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.3% as of May 2015 reflects tightening labor market
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with sustained employment gains
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal accelerating residential investment and forward-looking builder confidence, which increases demand for materials, labor, and consumer durables, translating to positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529k units in April 2015 shows recovery momentum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) in periods following HOUST acceleration versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero combined with stable inflation signals monetary accommodation that suppresses real discount rates and supports equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, forward SPX returns remain elevated due to reduced cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 216.4 as of May 2015
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns in accommodative regimes (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) do not exceed returns in tightening regimes by at least 200 bps on average, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and utilization, signaling robust aggregate supply and corporate profitability. Accelerating INDPRO over 3 months predicts positive SPX returns over the next 5 trading days as supply-side strength supports margin expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.44 in May 2015 near pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX 5-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.1%, t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal strong consumer demand and income growth. When both series accelerate simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to earnings upside, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The interaction captures demand-side momentum that neither series alone captures.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352.1B and PAYEMS at 131.0M in May 2015
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with employment and consumption growth
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression, or SPX 20-day returns in dual-acceleration regimes do not exceed single-series acceleration regimes by at least 150 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk, which triggers equity risk-off behavior and negative forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in one month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.3% in May 2015 with low volatility
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations. When CPI growth accelerates above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS rises, SPX forward returns turn negative over 60 days as terminal rate expectations increase.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.4 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% in May 2015
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15), or SPX 60-day returns in tightening regimes do not underperform accommodative regimes by at least 200 bps on average.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized signal robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns during expansion periods regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12990.3B in Q1 2015 reflects ongoing expansion
regime_hint: any regime with positive real GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day forward returns in high-growth quarters (GDPC1 > 2.5%) do not exceed low-growth quarters (GDPC1 < 1.5%) by at least 150 bps on average.
